steps.instruction:
Create a new backlog issue in .kiln/issues/. The user will have described the issue when they started this workflow — read the workflow activation context for their description.

1. If the description references a GitHub issue number (e.g., #42), fetch it: `gh issue view <number> --json title,body,labels,state`
2. Classify the issue:
   - Type: bug | friction | improvement | feature-request
   - Severity: blocking | high | medium | low
   - Category: skills | agents | hooks | templates | scaffold | workflow | other
   - Source: retro | manual | github-issue | pipeline-run
3. Check existing issues (from context) — if a duplicate exists with the same GitHub number or very similar title, report it and update instead of creating new.
4. Generate filename: YYYY-MM-DD-<short-slug>.md (today's date)
5. Create .kiln/issues/ directory if needed: `mkdir -p .kiln/issues`
6. Write the issue file with this frontmatter structure:
   ---
   title: "<title>"
   type: <type>
   severity: <severity>
   category: <category>
   source: <source>
   github_issue: <number or null>
   status: open
   date: YYYY-MM-DD
   ---
   ## Description
   <description text>
   ## Impact
   <impact if known>
   ## Suggested Fix
   <suggestion if known>
7. Write a confirmation summary to your output file listing the filename, type, severity, and category. Include the exact `.kiln/issues/<file>.md` path on its own line so the downstream parse step can pick it up.

You are the `dispatch-background-sync` step of `kiln:kiln-report-issue`. Your job is to fire-and-forget a background sub-agent that handles counter-gated reconciliation, then write the foreground user-facing output and stop. Do NOT wait for the sub-agent. Do NOT run shelf-sync or shelf-propose-manifest-improvement yourself.

## Step 1 — Use pre-resolved inputs

The wheel runtime has already resolved every value you need (see the `## Resolved Inputs` block prepended above). Use these placeholders directly — do NOT run `bash`, `jq`, `cat`, or `grep` to fetch them again:

- `CURRENT_COUNTER` = {{CURRENT_COUNTER}}
- `THRESHOLD` = {{THRESHOLD}}
- `ISSUE_FILE` = {{ISSUE_FILE}}
- `OBSIDIAN_PATH` = {{OBSIDIAN_PATH}}
- `SHELF_DIR` = {{SHELF_DIR}}

The next full-sync fires when the counter reaches `THRESHOLD - 1` on increment (i.e., at invocation `THRESHOLD - CURRENT_COUNTER` from now, or simply `THRESHOLD` if the counter is 0).

If either `ISSUE_FILE` or `OBSIDIAN_PATH` is missing or contains a placeholder string (which would have failed the resolver tripwire — so this should never happen post-FR-G3-5), fall back to `(unknown)` for foreground reporting per FR-010.

## Step 2 — Spawn the background sub-agent (fire-and-forget)

Call the `Agent` tool EXACTLY ONCE with:
- `subagent_type`: `general-purpose`
- `run_in_background`: **true**  (this is the critical flag that makes the spawn asynchronous)
- `description`: `kiln-report-issue background reconciliation`
- `prompt`: (verbatim — the SHELF_DIR placeholder is substituted by wheel into an absolute path before this step dispatches; the sub-agent reads its own inputs):

----BEGIN SUBAGENT PROMPT----
You are a background reconciliation sub-agent for /kiln:kiln-report-issue. Run these steps in order and exit. Do not report progress. Do not stall.

1. Run the consolidated counter-and-log step (FR-E2 batched wrapper). This single bash call replaces the previous 2-call chain (shelf-counter increment-and-decide + append-bg-log) — both actions live inside the wrapper now:

   `OUT=$(bash "{{SHELF_DIR}}/scripts/step-dispatch-background-sync.sh")`

   The wrapper emits per-action log lines on stderr-prefix `wheel:dispatch-background-sync: action=<n> | start|ok` and a final single-line JSON object on stdout matching:
   `{"step":"dispatch-background-sync","status":"ok","actions":["counter-increment-and-decide","append-bg-log"],"counter":{"before":N,"after":N,"threshold":N},"next_action":"increment|full-sync"}`
   On any failure inside the wrapper, `set -e` causes a non-zero exit and the failing action's `start` log line (with no matching `ok`) identifies which action failed.

2. Parse the wrapper JSON output:
   `ACTION=$(printf '%s' "$OUT" | tail -1 | jq -r '.next_action')`

3. If ACTION == "increment": exit 0.

4. If ACTION == "full-sync":
   a. Invoke the `/shelf:shelf-sync` skill. Wait for completion. If it errors, continue.
   b. Invoke the `/shelf:shelf-propose-manifest-improvement` skill. Wait for completion. If it errors, continue.
   c. Exit 0.

5. On any unhandled exception, append one more log line with action=error and short notes via `bash "{{SHELF_DIR}}/scripts/append-bg-log.sh" 0 0 0 error "<short reason>"`, then exit 0. Never hang. Never retry.
----END SUBAGENT PROMPT----

After the `Agent` tool call returns, DO NOT call any more tools. (The tool call returns synchronously — the sub-agent is launched asynchronously because `run_in_background: true`.)

## Step 3 — Write foreground user-facing output (FR-010)

Compute `NEXT_FULL_SYNC_AT`:
- If `CURRENT_COUNTER == 0`: `NEXT_FULL_SYNC_AT = THRESHOLD`
- Otherwise: `NEXT_FULL_SYNC_AT = THRESHOLD - CURRENT_COUNTER`

Write the following three lines (plus a trailing newline) to the step's output file `.wheel/outputs/dispatch-background-sync.txt`:

```
issue_file: <ISSUE_FILE>
obsidian_path: <OBSIDIAN_PATH>
background sync queued — full reconciliation next at invocation <NEXT_FULL_SYNC_AT>/<THRESHOLD>
```

Example when counter=3, threshold=10:
```
issue_file: .kiln/issues/2026-04-22-slow-report.md
obsidian_path: @second-brain/projects/ai-repo-template/issues/2026-04-22-slow-report.md
background sync queued — full reconciliation next at invocation 7/10
```

## Step 4 — Stop

The step is terminal. DO NOT read the sub-agent's output. DO NOT call Sleep. DO NOT call any further tools. The wheel engine treats this step as complete once the output file exists.

## Fallback note (for maintainers)

If Phase H smoke testing shows that `run_in_background: true` inside this wheel agent-step does NOT actually return control immediately (e.g., the wheel waits on the sub-agent to finish), the designated fallback per `specs/report-issue-speedup/plan.md` §Unknown 1 is to replace this step with a `type: command` step that uses `nohup ... &; disown`. Fall back only if empirical evidence forces it; this path is the primary.

## Cross-plugin script resolution (Theme F5 — closed; SHELF_DIR via $plugin(shelf))

The `SHELF_DIR` input above is resolved at hook-time by `plugin-wheel/lib/resolve_inputs.sh::resolve_inputs` against the session registry built by `build_session_registry`. By the time you read this instruction, the literal absolute path to the shelf install (e.g. under marketplace cache or under a `--plugin-dir` override) is already in the prompt — both in the `## Resolved Inputs` block and substituted into every `{{SHELF_DIR}}` placeholder in this body. The pre-flight resolver fails loud at activation if shelf is not enabled in this session (FR-F3-3). The cross-plugin gap (where the legacy calling-plugin token resolved to plugin-kiln while the scripts actually lived in plugin-shelf) is structurally closed by `requires_plugins:["shelf"]` + `inputs: { SHELF_DIR: $plugin(shelf) }`. See `specs/cross-plugin-resolver-and-preflight-registry/spec.md` and `specs/wheel-step-input-output-schema/spec.md` for the full design.

set -e
SCRIPT_BASE="plugin-kiln/scripts/pi-apply"
FETCH="$SCRIPT_BASE/fetch-retro-issues.sh"
PARSE="$SCRIPT_BASE/parse-pi-blocks.sh"
CLASSIFY="$SCRIPT_BASE/classify-pi-status.sh"
RENDER="$SCRIPT_BASE/render-pi-diff.sh"
HASH="$SCRIPT_BASE/compute-pi-hash.sh"
EMIT="$SCRIPT_BASE/emit-report.sh"

# Step 1
ISSUES_JSON=$(bash "$FETCH")
ISSUE_COUNT=$(printf '%s' "$ISSUES_JSON" | jq 'length')
echo "fetched: $ISSUE_COUNT issues" >&2

# Step 2
BLOCKS_NDJSON=""
if [[ "$ISSUE_COUNT" -gt 0 ]]; then
  for i in $(seq 0 $((ISSUE_COUNT - 1))); do
    NUMBER=$(printf '%s' "$ISSUES_JSON" | jq -r ".[$i].number")
    URL=$(printf '%s' "$ISSUES_JSON" | jq -r ".[$i].url")
    BODY=$(printf '%s' "$ISSUES_JSON" | jq -r ".[$i].body")
    PARSED=$(printf '%s' "$BODY" | bash "$PARSE" "$NUMBER" || true)
    [[ -z "$PARSED" ]] && continue
    ENRICHED=$(printf '%s\n' "$PARSED" | jq -c --arg url "$URL" '. + {issue_url: $url}')
    BLOCKS_NDJSON+="$ENRICHED"$'\n'
  done
fi
BLOCKS_NDJSON="${BLOCKS_NDJSON%$'\n'}"
BLOCK_COUNT=$(printf '%s' "$BLOCKS_NDJSON" | grep -c . || echo 0)
echo "parsed: $BLOCK_COUNT PI blocks" >&2

# Step 3
CLASSIFIED_NDJSON=""
if [[ -n "$BLOCKS_NDJSON" ]]; then
  while IFS= read -r rec; do
    [[ -z "$rec" ]] && continue
    IS_ERR=$(printf '%s' "$rec" | jq -r 'has("parse_error")')
    if [[ "$IS_ERR" == "true" ]]; then CLASSIFIED_NDJSON+="$rec"$'\n'; continue; fi
    FILE=$(printf '%s' "$rec" | jq -r '.file')
    ANCHOR=$(printf '%s' "$rec" | jq -r '.anchor')
    PROPOSED=$(printf '%s' "$rec" | jq -r '.proposed')
    STATUS=$(bash "$CLASSIFY" --target-file "$FILE" --target-anchor "$ANCHOR" --proposed "$PROPOSED")
    CLASSIFIED=$(printf '%s' "$rec" | jq -c --arg s "$STATUS" '. + {status: $s}')
    CLASSIFIED_NDJSON+="$CLASSIFIED"$'\n'
  done <<<"$BLOCKS_NDJSON"
  CLASSIFIED_NDJSON="${CLASSIFIED_NDJSON%$'\n'}"
fi

# Step 4
FINAL_NDJSON=""
if [[ -n "$CLASSIFIED_NDJSON" ]]; then
  while IFS= read -r rec; do
    [[ -z "$rec" ]] && continue
    IS_ERR=$(printf '%s' "$rec" | jq -r 'has("parse_error")')
    if [[ "$IS_ERR" == "true" ]]; then FINAL_NDJSON+="$rec"$'\n'; continue; fi
    ISSUE=$(printf '%s' "$rec" | jq -r '.issue_number')
    FILE=$(printf '%s' "$rec" | jq -r '.file')
    ANCHOR=$(printf '%s' "$rec" | jq -r '.anchor')
    CURRENT=$(printf '%s' "$rec" | jq -r '.current')
    PROPOSED=$(printf '%s' "$rec" | jq -r '.proposed')
    STATUS=$(printf '%s' "$rec" | jq -r '.status')
    DIFF=""
    if [[ "$STATUS" == "actionable" ]]; then
      DIFF=$(bash "$RENDER" --target-file "$FILE" --target-anchor "$ANCHOR" --current "$CURRENT" --proposed "$PROPOSED") || DIFF=""
    fi
    HASH_INPUT="$DIFF"
    [[ "$STATUS" != "actionable" ]] && HASH_INPUT="$PROPOSED"
    PI_HASH=$(bash "$HASH" --issue-number "$ISSUE" --target-file "$FILE" --target-anchor "$ANCHOR" --proposed-diff "$HASH_INPUT")
    if [[ -n "$DIFF" ]]; then
      ENRICHED=$(printf '%s' "$rec" | jq -c --arg d "$DIFF" --arg h "$PI_HASH" '. + {diff: $d, pi_hash: $h}')
    else
      ENRICHED=$(printf '%s' "$rec" | jq -c --arg h "$PI_HASH" '. + {pi_hash: $h}')
    fi
    FINAL_NDJSON+="$ENRICHED"$'\n'
  done <<<"$CLASSIFIED_NDJSON"
  FINAL_NDJSON="${FINAL_NDJSON%$'\n'}"
fi

# Step 5
REPORT_PATH=$(printf '%s' "$FINAL_NDJSON" | bash "$EMIT")

# Step 6
N_ACT=$(printf '%s' "$FINAL_NDJSON" | jq -c 'select(.status == "actionable")' | grep -c . || true)
N_APP=$(printf '%s' "$FINAL_NDJSON" | jq -c 'select(.status == "already-applied")' | grep -c . || true)
N_STA=$(printf '%s' "$FINAL_NDJSON" | jq -c 'select(.status == "stale")' | grep -c . || true)

for i in $(seq 0 $((ISSUE_COUNT - 1))); do
  NUMBER=$(printf '%s' "$ISSUES_JSON" | jq -r ".[$i].number")
  URL=$(printf '%s' "$ISSUES_JSON" | jq -r ".[$i].url")
  BODY=$(printf '%s' "$ISSUES_JSON" | jq -r ".[$i].body")
  PARSED=$(printf '%s' "$BODY" | bash "$PARSE" "$NUMBER" || true)
  [[ -z "$PARSED" ]] && continue
  ENRICHED=$(printf '%s\n' "$PARSED" | jq -c --arg url "$URL" '. + {issue_url: $url}')
  BLOCKS_NDJSON+="$ENRICHED"$'\n'

echo "parsed: $BLOCK_COUNT PI blocks" >&2

CLASSIFIED_NDJSON=""
while IFS= read -r rec; do
  [[ -z "$rec" ]] && continue
  IS_ERR=$(printf '%s' "$rec" | jq -r 'has("parse_error")')
  if [[ "$IS_ERR" == "true" ]]; then CLASSIFIED_NDJSON+="$rec"$'\n'; continue; fi
  FILE=$(printf '%s' "$rec" | jq -r '.file')
  ANCHOR=$(printf '%s' "$rec" | jq -r '.anchor')
  PROPOSED=$(printf '%s' "$rec" | jq -r '.proposed')
  STATUS=$(bash "$CLASSIFY" --target-file "$FILE" --target-anchor "$ANCHOR" --proposed "$PROPOSED")
  CLASSIFIED=$(printf '%s' "$rec" | jq -c --arg s "$STATUS" '. + {status: $s}')
  CLASSIFIED_NDJSON+="$CLASSIFIED"$'\n'
done <<<"$BLOCKS_NDJSON"
CLASSIFIED_NDJSON="${CLASSIFIED_NDJSON%$'\n'}"

FINAL_NDJSON=""
while IFS= read -r rec; do
  [[ -z "$rec" ]] && continue
  IS_ERR=$(printf '%s' "$rec" | jq -r 'has("parse_error")')
  if [[ "$IS_ERR" == "true" ]]; then FINAL_NDJSON+="$rec"$'\n'; continue; fi
  ISSUE=$(printf '%s' "$rec" | jq -r '.issue_number')
  FILE=$(printf '%s' "$rec" | jq -r '.file')
  ANCHOR=$(printf '%s' "$rec" | jq -r '.anchor')
  CURRENT=$(printf '%s' "$rec" | jq -r '.current')
  PROPOSED=$(printf '%s' "$rec" | jq -r '.proposed')
  STATUS=$(printf '%s' "$rec" | jq -r '.status')
  DIFF=""
  if [[ "$STATUS" == "actionable" ]]; then
    DIFF=$(bash "$RENDER" --target-file "$FILE" --target-anchor "$ANCHOR" --current "$CURRENT" --proposed "$PROPOSED") || DIFF=""
  fi
  HASH_INPUT="$DIFF"
  [[ "$STATUS" != "actionable" ]] && HASH_INPUT="$PROPOSED"
  PI_HASH=$(bash "$HASH" --issue-number "$ISSUE" --target-file "$FILE" --target-anchor "$ANCHOR" --proposed-diff "$HASH_INPUT")
  if [[ -n "$DIFF" ]]; then

  else
    ENRICHED=$(printf '%s' "$rec" | jq -c --arg h "$PI_HASH" '. + {pi_hash: $h}')
  fi
  FINAL_NDJSON+="$ENRICHED"$'\n'
done <<<"$CLASSIFIED_NDJSON"

Discovered while running `/kiln:kiln-pi-apply` against retros from PR #166 and PR #168. Two independent bugs that together quietly broke the retro → source feedback loop for the last two pipelines.

## Bug 1 — Label filter mismatch

`plugin-kiln/scripts/pi-apply/fetch-retro-issues.sh` filters by `--label retrospective`:

```bash
gh issue list --label retrospective --state open --json number,url,title,body --limit 100
```

But the retrospective agent in `plugin-kiln/skills/kiln-build-prd/SKILL.md` files retro issues with `--label "build-prd"` only — no `retrospective` label.

**Result**: every retro from `/kiln:kiln-build-prd` since this convention was established has been invisible to `/kiln:kiln-pi-apply`. Issues #167 and #169 (this week) both shipped with only `build-prd`. They were silently excluded from pi-apply runs until I manually re-labeled them.

**Fix**: in `plugin-kiln/skills/kiln-build-prd/SKILL.md`, the retrospective agent's `gh issue create` invocation needs `--label "build-prd,retrospective"`.

## Bug 2 — Format mismatch between retro output and parser input

`plugin-kiln/scripts/pi-apply/parse-pi-blocks.sh` expects PI blocks shaped:

```
### PI-N
**File**: <path>
**Current**: <text>
**Proposed**: <text>
**Why**: <text>
```

(per contract `specs/workflow-governance/contracts/interfaces.md` Module 3 §parse-pi-blocks.sh — bold-inline markers).

But the retrospective agent prompt in `plugin-kiln/skills/kiln-build-prd/SKILL.md` shows this template:

```
File: [path]
Current: "[exact text]"
Proposed: "[rewritten text]"
Why: [one sentence]
```

— no bold markers, and retro agents (correctly following the prompt) wrap the whole thing in a triple-backtick code fence.

**Result**: even after I fixed bug 1 by re-labeling #167 + #169, pi-apply parsed 20 PI blocks → **18 parse errors, 2 stale, 0 actionable** because the parser couldn't find any `**File**:` markers.

**Fix options** (pick one):
- Update `kiln-build-prd` SKILL.md retro prompt to use the bold-inline format the parser expects (canonical per contract).
- Update `parse-pi-blocks.sh` to also accept the unbold-in-code-fence format (relaxes the contract).
- Both formats supported, with the contract documenting both.

## Combined impact

- Issues #167 (PR #166 retro) and #169 (PR #168 retro) — 16 PI blocks total — were never seen by pi-apply.
- The recurring B-1 substrate gap (which #169 PI-2 explicitly asked be promoted to a roadmap item) had to be promoted manually rather than discovered via pi-apply.
- Same shape as #169 PI-4: "PRD success metrics must be framed against the substrate that will measure them" — here, the retrospective output format must match the substrate (pi-apply parser) that consumes it. The skill (kiln-build-prd) and the substrate (pi-apply) disagreed on the contract; nobody noticed because pi-apply silently emitted "0 actionable" on every run.

## Suggested follow-up actions

1. Fix bug 1 immediately (one-line edit to the retrospective agent's `gh issue create` invocation).
2. Pick a format for bug 2 and fix one side. Recommend: keep the retro prompt format (it's friendlier to write) and relax the parser to accept it. The bold-inline format buys nothing — the parser knows the structure either way.
3. Backfill: re-edit issues #167 and #169 to either format once chosen, or accept that those two retros are manually applied (manual-review report at `.kiln/logs/pi-manual-review-2026-04-25T11-13-13Z.md`).
4. Add a `/kiln:kiln-pi-apply` smoke test fixture under `plugin-kiln/tests/` that catches the format/label mismatch on next regression.
